title: ocellochloa chapadensis
intro: Ocellochloa chapadensis is a type of plant species that belongs to the Poaceae family, commonly known as a type of grass. It is native to South America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting the immune system. In traditional medicine, Ocellochloa chapadensis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is used to make a topical cream or ointment for external applications.
